title: Meta to put its custom Iris AI chip into production in September, targeting 14 GW compute footprint
subtitle: An internal memo obtained by Reuters says the fourth-generation MTIA design cleared testing in six weeks and will move to TSMC production in September, aiming to roughly double Meta's compute capacity next year.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Meta plans to begin manufacturing its custom AI accelerator, code-named Iris, in September 2026, according to an internal memo reviewed by Reuters and published July 9–10. Iris is the fourth generation of Meta's in-house Meta Training and Inference Accelerator line (MTIA), designed with Broadcom and fabricated at TSMC. The memo says testing took about six weeks and surfaced no major issues, unusually fast for a leading-edge custom silicon program.

The chip is part of a plan to grow Meta's total AI compute capacity to roughly 14 gigawatts in 2027, roughly double the current footprint. The strategic point is cost: Meta uses hundreds of thousands of Nvidia GPUs today, and the delta between an in-house accelerator and a merchant GPU compounds fast at that scale. Even if Iris is only competitive for a subset of Meta's workloads — ads ranking, recommendation, some inference — displacing GPU spend on those workloads is enough to fund the next generation of the program.

Meta joins a widening list of hyperscalers that now design their own AI silicon: Google (TPU), Amazon (Trainium and Inferentia), Microsoft (Maia), OpenAI (the Broadcom-designed 'Jalapeño' inference chip announced in June), and now Meta. Broadcom is the common thread on almost every non-Google program, which is part of why its shares have re-rated with the AI capex cycle. Nvidia is not being replaced — it is being fenced in to the training workloads where its software moat holds hardest.

Takeaway for learners: 'Nvidia dominates AI' is a headline, not an analysis. The real structure is that Nvidia dominates training, TSMC dominates fabrication, Broadcom dominates the custom-ASIC design services layer, and each hyperscaler is trying to insource inference. If you want to understand the AI stack in 2026, learn the difference between training and inference — because that is the boundary the industry is now organizing around.

sources.title: Meta's new AI chips will begin production in September — TechCrunch
Exclusive: Meta to put AI chip into production in September as it looks to double computing capacity, memo shows — Reuters via Yahoo
